title: Contract No. 32500098 Amendment: Pacific Site Management: Landscaping Services for Public Works Department

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or his designee to execute an
amendment to Contract No. 32500098 with Pacific Site Management to increase the
spending authority by $150,000 for a new total not to exceed $300,000 and extend the
contract through June 30, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the contract amendment is primarily available in the FY 2025 budget in the
Clean Storm and Zero Waste funds. Additional funding will be available for projects as
they arise in other funds, including the Building Maintenance Fund, General Fund,
Streetlight Assessment District Fund, Off Street Parking Fund, Measure BB Local
Streets & Roads Fund, and Sanitary Sewer Fund.
sections.current_situation:
The current contract, Contract No. 32500098, approved by Council through Resolution
No. 70,804-N.S. on September 30, 2024, is managed by the Public Works Department
(PW) to address onsite needs at City facilities. PW is amending the existing contract to
add funds to cover ongoing services and future projects, such as the additional need to
retrofit and provide maintenance to several green infrastructure locations.
The City relies in part on green infrastructure (GI) systems—comprised of stormwater
management practices (SMPs) such as bioretention, bioswales, and permeable
pavement—to reduce stormwater volume and pollutants delivered to local waterways.
Because GI systems incorporate vegetation, they can change as plant communities
grow and establish. An effective and rigorous maintenance program is crucial for the
long-term sustainability and function of GI systems.
Typical maintenance tasks include removing competitive species, removing sediment,
trash, and debris, and maintaining irrigation, and other GI components. Maintenance

tasks may also include a range of other activities including repairing small erosion
problems, pruning trees, removing graffiti, and replanting or reseeding areas.
Currently, the City has a total of 43 GI installations. The attached list of sites shows the
anticipated locations and maintenance frequency that Pacific Site Management will be
contracted with to perform services if contract is approved. This includes sites that
Public Works has maintenance oversight and management responsibility.
Traffic calming devices, such as traffic circles, often contain vegetation which, when
overgrown, can obstruct sight lines and right-o
sections.background:
PW does not have staff assigned to perform landscape maintenance. PW’s request is to
amend their existing contract with Pacific Site Management to cover additional work at
traffic circles, green infrastructure, and additional department facilities.
In April 2023, under Specification No. 23-11564, the City released a Request for
Proposals (RFP) and convened a panel of stakeholders that selected Pacific Site
Management as the most responsive and responsible bidder for providing landscaping
services at City facilities managed by HHCS. These same standards and landscaping
services are immediately applicable to the services required to meet the City’s traffic
circle and green infrastructure maintenance needs. PW piggybacked off of this
solicitation to execute the contract.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no anticipated negative environmental effects or climate impacts of this
action. This contract amendment will help ensure proper maintenance of traffic circles
and green infrastructure installations, which slow runoff by dispersing it to vegetated
areas, which promote infiltration and evapotranspiration, and/or use bioretention to
improve the water quality of stormwater runoff. In addition, any replacement plantings or
new landscaping will be Bay-Friendly, drought-tolerant, and pollinator-supportive. Any
vegetative debris generated from their services will be converted into usable compost.

sections.rationale:
Pacific Site Management is a landscaping company with demonstrated experience in
providing landscaping services for many clients with multiple large facilities. Their RFP
proposal included services that align with the needed maintenance services identified at
various PW locations. Approving this amendment saves staff time and City funds by
avoiding another RFP process for services identical to those outlined in Specification
No. 23-11564
sections.alternatives:
There is no alternative action at this time. If the contract amendment is not authorized,
there are no internal staff available to complete this type of work.
CONTACT PERSON
Joseph Griffin, Community Services Specialist, Public Works, 510-981-6358

Page 3

RESOLUTION NO. 70,804-N.S.
CONTRACT AMENDMENT: PACIFIC SITE MANAGEMENT: LANDSCAPING
SERVICES FOR PUBLIC WORKS
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley Department of Public Works, is committed to providing
state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities; and
WHEREAS, the City Council approved Resolution No.64,507-N.S. on June 3, 2009,
establishing a Bay-Friendly landscaping policy for City projects; and
WHEREAS, the City Council approved Resolution No.69,090-N.S. on September 10,
2019 adopting a Green Infrastructure Plan; and
WHEREAS, Pacific Site Management was selected as the most qualified vendor by the
City’s Request For a Proposal process to meet a Bay-Friendly, Green Infrastructure
Plan in April 2023, under Specification No. 23-11564, which included bidding from
multiple vendors, draft proposals, and pricing; and
WHEREAS, Pacific Site Management and the City executed Contract No. 32500098,
which was approved by Council on September 30, 2024, through Resolution No. 70,804-
N.S. for an amount not to exceed $150,000; and
WHEREAS, with additional landscaping service needs throughout the City now evident
for services outlined under Specification No. 23-11564, funds are available to meet these
needs in Public Works budgets for FY25 through FY26, including the Zero Waste Fund
601 and Clean Storm Fund 616.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or his designee is authorized to execute a contract amendment, with
Pacific Site Management to provide landscaping services for Public Works locations in
an amount not to exceed $300,000 through June 30, 2027. A record signature copy of
said contract and any amendments will be on file in the City Clerk Department.
